findings.evidence: Body is `...mapSubgroup`; `MulEquiv.mapSubgroup : G ≃* H → Subgroup G ≃o Subgroup H` at mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Map.lean:433.
findings.fix: Drop the alias and use `(fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfPath γ).mapSubgroup` directly, or justify the name as more than notation.
findings.issue: `basepointChangeSubgroupOrderIso γ` is definitionally `(fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfPath γ).mapSubgroup` — a pure rename of existing `MulEquiv.mapSubgroup` with no added structure.

findings.evidence: Each proof is a one-liner citing `Subgroup.map_inf`/`map_sup`/`map_iSup`/`map_mono`/`map_bot` or `(orderIso).le_iff_le`; all are instances available on any `MulEquiv.mapSubgroup`.
findings.fix: Derive these from the `mapSubgroup` OrderIso (e.g. `OrderIso.map_inf`/`map_iSup`, `OrderIso.le_iff_le`, `OrderIso.injective`) rather than re-exposing each as a named transport lemma, keeping only the genuinely new transport + membership + `normal_*_iff` API the roadmap needs.
findings.issue: The order/lattice block (`_mono`, `_le_..._iff`, `_injective`, `_inf`, `_sup`, `_iSup`, `_bot`, `_top`) restates the generic `OrderIso`/`Subgroup.map` lemmas for one fixed equivalence — parallel API differing only by naming.

summary: Proofs correctly reuse Mathlib generics (no reinvention), but a large fraction of the file is parallel API over `MulEquiv.mapSubgroup`/`subgroupMap` specialized to one equivalence; the order-iso def is a pure alias and the order/lattice lemmas are free instances of the generic OrderIso/Subgroup.map API.
